observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC strictly comply with the requirement of invitation in all stages of the procurement to enhance transparency in the procurement process of the Municipality as intended by law.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management at time of report.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the non-implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the
Municipality’s PPE and prepare and submit the required report thereon, in
proper form and within the prescribed period;
b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that PPE Ledger Cards were being maintained
and accomplish the column related to it in the RPCPPE;
c. Inventory Committee to start the reconciliation of the Municipality’s PPE per
Inventory report with the records maintained by the Offices of the Accounting
and the GSO; and henceforth,
d. Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the
provisions of paragraphs 1 and 2, Sections 120 and 124, respectively, of the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, Section 53, Chapter III of the GAAM, Volume III
and paragraph C.3, Chapter 5 of the Manual on Property Custodianship.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the 362 DVs without further
delay. Otherwise, the said transactions without DVs and supporting
documents shall be disallowed in audit; and henceforth,
b. Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with
Section 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No.
2009-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to
conduct the reconciliation of their records pertaining to the Cash Local
Treasury account, to establish the details of the difference between their
records;
b. OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary
corrections in their records so as to properly reflect the correct balances of
the accounts; and
c. concerned municipal officials, to henceforth, ensure compliance with Section
181(c) of the GAAM, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. recognize in the proper account the Municipality’s share in the LLDA fishpen
fees;
b. prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to effect the reclassification of the LLDA fund
to Share from National Wealth in the General Fund from Other Payables
account in the Trust Fund to present the correct balance of the affected
accounts; and henceforth,
c. strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 15, 2015 and
BLGF Memorandum Circular No. 03-01-2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. analyze and determine the cause/s of the variance in the reciprocal accounts;
b. effect the necessary adjustments to present the correct balances of the
accounts; and henceforth,
c. ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts are at all times equal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. record the fund transfer in the book of accounts as a Receivable under the Due
from NGOs/POs account;
b. demand from the PMECC officials the submission of the lacking supporting
documents;
c. require the PMECC to submit the Liquidation Report and Fund Utilization
Report for the amount granted by the Municipality and review the expenditures
if these conform to the intended purpose and submit the same to the Audit
Team for verification; and henceforth,
d. strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2007-001 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445
as regard funds transfers to NGOs/POs.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant to: a.1 continue to reconcile, analyze and investigate, together with the General Services Office, the noted discrepancies between the book balance and the RPCPPE to identify the cause/s of deficiencies and make necessary adjustments; a.2 exert efforts to gather records and documents relative to the PPEs with no exact acquisition dates and other important details/information for the accurate computation of depreciation; a.3 review the Schedule of PPE and make adjustments in the depreciation to fairly present the financial statements in accordance with PAG 3 of IPSAS 17; a.4 provide residual value estimates to at least five percent (5%) in accordance with PAG 6 of IPSAS 17 to the identified PPEs with no residual value provision; and b. Municipal General Services Office (GSO) to: b.1 ensure the accuracy and completeness of RPCPPE by reconciling first year property and accounting records to come up with reconciled schedule/master list of properties; b.2 based on the reconciled accounts schedule/master list of properties add new acquisition and deduct disposals to come up with a new schedule to be used as basis in the conduct of the physical count; and b.3 coordinate with Accounting Office to ensure that the newly prepared RPCPPE and accounting records are reconciled.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office has already updated the Ledger Card of various PPEs, while the GSO has started updating the PAR to ensure proper source documents for basis of RPCPPE. The Accounting Office is continuously exerting efforts to gather the necessary documents in order to provide accurate computation of depreciation. Over depreciation was reconciled and adjusted in July 2021 records. The Municipal Accounting Office already applied the 5% residual value for PPEs purchased in CYs 2020 and 2021.
observations.reason: Management has yet to reconcile the books and RPCPPE due to issues with records and available documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records regularly and effect the necessary adjustments to avoid accumulation thereof and come up with the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in their respective records. Furthermore, both the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to locate the missing documents to support adjustments in the Cashbook.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant initiated reconciliation; reconciliation was put in place to ensure accuracy of the Cashbooks and Ledger balances.
observations.reason: Reconciliation for the Cash - Local Treasury is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal General Services Officer to: a.1 conduct a complete physical count of properties specifically land; a.2 prepare and reconcile the RPCPPE with accounting records to ensure correct balances; a.3 exert effort to locate the missing TCTs or take the necessary steps for the titling of the land owned by the Municipality for verification/validation and submit to the Audit Team certified copies of the same; and a.4 henceforth, ensure that the RPCPPE contains all the details of the property specifically TCT Numbers for easy reference. b. Municipal Accountant to: b.1 ensure the accounting records and the RPCPPE are reconciled; and b.2 exert effort to gather documents and records relative to the untitled and undocumented parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office has already updated RPCPPE and coordinated with the Assessor Office in identifying the land under the name of the Municipality. Supporting documents and initial schedule, as well as copies of 4 TCT documents were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Management yet to reconcile the books and RPCPPE due to issues with records and available documents; lack of readily available documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the Chairman of the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to prepare and maintain an Inventory of all Local Roads and a Road Map of the Municipality with the corresponding Road Network ID Numbers and Road Names in order to observe the yearly preparation of Report of Local Road Network; b. create a sub-committee under the Municipal Inventory Committee that will primarily handle the inventory of Local Road Network System; and c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to recognize the changes upon completion of the required inventory reports and validation of accounting records. Further, depreciation and salvage value of the assets shall be provided accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Inventory Committee, through its Secretariat, has established procurement of infrastructure projects and the BAC Secretariat already inquired with the GPPB for the process of mandatory posting on the GPPB Online Portal. The initial list was provided to the Accounting Officer.
observations.reason: Management has prepared and maintained an Inventory in the Municipal part but incomplete implementation of the audit recommendation; no full implementation reason given.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancies of the reciprocal accounts and subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the true balance of the said accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality. Likewise, initiate the collection and settlement for the receivables and payables, respectively, and to reconcile and settle the valid balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has reconciled the Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs balances of prior years; reconciliations also include unreconciled balances with the Barangay.
observations.reason: Due to LGUs and Due from LGUs still have balances for reconciliation for CY 2018 and 2019; prior years’ balances still subject for further reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to produce and submit to the Audit Team all the unsubmitted 492 DVs without further delay. Otherwise, the said transactions without DVs and supporting documents shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted 130 vouchers amounting to P3,883,973.67; efforts to comply with the submission of DVs for previous years’ transactions are being made. The Offices of the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer are monitoring submission of current year documents.
observations.reason: Management is still having difficulty retrieving the remaining unsubmitted 362 DVs amounting to P17,530,605.56.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC through its Secretariat to ensure compliance with the posting requirements of all procurement in accordance with GPPB TSO Advisory No. 05-2020; and Municipal Accountant to ensure that claims/disbursements are duly supported with complete documentation before processing for payment as required under Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445. Also, ensure perpetual inventory method be observed and all payments to suppliers/contractors be made by check.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat has established the required posting; the Municipal Accounting Office adopted the perpetual inventory method and is ensuring suppliers/contractors are paid by check. The BAC Secretariat inquired with the GPPB for the mandatory posting process.
observations.reason: Access to GPPB posting was established later; some implementation steps are recent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. Municipal Accountant to require the attachment of approved payrolls or list of payees indicating their net payments before the grant of cash advance and required supporting documents attached to the Liquidation Report to substantiate the liquidation; and b. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to submit the necessary documentary requirements as required under DSWD MC No. 04 to facilitate post audit. Otherwise, the same shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required documents to the Audit Team and MSWD submitted the Liquidation Report for SAP Distribution in 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare Development Office to prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances as well as the required one-time report for in-kind donations and submit the same to the NDRRMC through the OCD and furnish the Audit Team; and ensure proper monitoring of all donations in-kind by maintaining separate records of receipts and issuance/distribution thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Summary/List of Donations and the required one-time report for in-kind donations were prepared and submitted to the NDRRM Council through the OCD on June 3, 2021. Management installed measures to properly monitor all donations received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to explain in writing the award of contracts/POs despite absence of supporting documents and submit certified copies of the said lacking documents to the Audit Team for post audit; ensure posting of contracts and related notices in the GPPB Online Portal; and Accounting Office to conduct proper review of documents prior to certification on the propriety of payments and refrain from processing transactions with incomplete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the lacking documents to the Audit Team. The Accounting Office will comply and do proper review prior to certification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Budget Officer for prompt submission of the SAAOB to the Audit Team; b. BAC Secretariat to post all awarded contracts in the GPPB Portal; and c. concerned Municipal Officials to immediately submit to the Audit Team all the required documents related to SAP and COVID-19 expenses, otherwise transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted required SAAOB documents to the Audit Team on May 6, 2021. The BAC Secretariat inquired with GPPB for posting; Municipal Accountant installed measures for review of supporting documents.
observations.reason: Submission of CY 2021 SAAOB was beyond the prescribed period; partial implementation only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all accountable officers of the Municipality be properly bonded pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-009.
observations.managementAction: The Certificate of Fidelity Bond from the Bureau of Treasury for the Municipal Mayor and the GSO Head as the Property Custodian was secured by Management and a copy was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource Management Officer and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to adopt an ATM payroll system through Authorized Government Depository Banks to eliminate the risk of loss or misapplication of funds aside from promoting transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: As of Sept 7, 2021, 234 out of 274 or 85% of the Municipality’s Job Order Employees have their ATM payroll account for the payment of their wages and allowances. The Municipality has installed ATM payroll for those employees.
observations.reason: The remaining employees not yet enrolled in ATM payroll; full transition ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned Municipal Officials submit immediately to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents to substantiate the disbursements made; and Municipal Accountant to conduct proper review of documents prior to certification on the propriety of payments to be made, and refrain from further processing of transactions with incomplete documentation and/or those non-compliant with procurement laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required supporting documents to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant has installed measures for proper review of documents prior to certification on the propriety of payments and shall refrain from processing transactions with incomplete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. prioritize the reconciliation of the accounts Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth to identify the status/composition of the unremitted balance and remit immediately the remittances to the various agencies if warranted; b. make representations with the concerned agencies as regards the settlement of the prior years' unremitted withheld taxes/contributions/payments to avoid imposition of interest, surcharges and penalties; and c. henceforth, strictly adhere with the prescribed period of remittances of the withheld taxes, contribution/loan payments to the various agencies to avoid the payment of interest, surcharges and penalties and non-granting of benefits.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prioritized reconciliation of ledger against schedule to update the prior period records and filed with GSIS the request for update and remittances. Monthly reconciliation of schedules is being conducted to ensure remittances are made in full and within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Partial implementation; some prior period balances remain subject to update and reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to continue to develop and implement GAD-related programs/projects/activities with advocacy on women empowerment, promote gender equality and improve the welfare of women, and study on how to maximize the utilization of the GAD fund; and to ensure GAD funds be used as intended especially during the pandemic.
observations.managementAction: A meeting was conducted on May 24, 2021 to ensure all GAD-related programs will be implemented. The Municipality hired a consultant to guide the GAD Focal Person in planning appropriate PPAs.
observations.reason: Partial implementation; no amended GPB submitted at time of report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to: a. formulate and submit a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) incorporating prior years’ unexpended balances of the LDRRMF in accordance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF including the utilization of the Continuing Appropriation and Special Trust Fund and submit the same before the 15th day after the end of each month through the MDRRMC and MDC to the Audit Team; and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers and Equipment Ledger Cards for each type of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment as required.
observations.managementAction: Prior years’ unexpended balances were incorporated in the LDRRMFIP and 2021 reports. Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF and related ledger cards were submitted on time. Subsidiary ledgers and equipment ledger cards maintained for each type of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned Municipal Officials to: submit copies of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, together with pertinent documents which are necessary in the conduct of auditorial review, and in the technical evaluation thereof.
observations.managementAction: Copies of contracts and purchase orders are still not submitted to the Audit Team for review despite requests.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned Municipal Officials to: a. prepare the Local Council for the Protection of Children Annual Work and Financial Plan as required by the abovementioned law, rules and regulations; and b. submit the Accomplishment Report on the implementation of LCPC PPAs in the prescribed form to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Planning Office and the Municipal Accountant held a video conference meeting whereas the said AOM was discussed. The said AOM was discussed. The LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan and Accomplishment Report will be prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to:

a.1 continue to reconcile, analyze and investigate, together with General
Services Office, the noted discrepancies between the book balance and the
RPCPPE to fully identify the cause/s of deficiencies and make necessary
adjustments;

a.2 exert efforts to gather records and documents relative to PPEs with no exact
acquisition dates and other important details/information for the accurate
computation of depreciation;

a.3 review the Schedule of PPE and make adjustments in the depreciation to
fairly present the financial statements in accordance with PAG 3 of IPSAS
17;

a.4 provide residual value estimates to at least five percent (5%) in accordance
with PAG 6 of IPSAS 17 to the identified PPEs with no residual value
provision; and

b. Municipal General Services Office (GSO) to:

b.1 ensure the accuracy and completeness of RPCPPE by reconciling first prior
year property and accounting records to come up with reconciled
schedule/master list of properties;

b.2 based on the reconciled schedule/master list of properties add new
acquisition and deduct disposals to come up with a new schedule to be used
as basis in the conduct of the physical count; and

b.3 coordinate with Accounting Office to ensure that the newly prepared
RPCPPE and accounting records are reconciled.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the
OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records regularly and effect the
necessary adjustments to avoid accumulation thereof and come up with the correct
balance of the Cash in Bank account in their respective records. Furthermore, both
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to locate
the missing documents to support adjustments in the Cashbook.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal General Services Officer to:

a.1 conduct a complete physical count of properties specifically land;

a.2 prepare and reconcile the RPCPPE with accounting records to ensure
correct balances;

a.3 exert effort to locate the missing TCTs or take the necessary steps for the
titling of the land owned by the Municipality for verification/validation and
submit to the Audit Team certified copies of the same; and

a.4 henceforth, ensure that the RPCPPE contains all the details of the property
specifically TCT Numbers for easy reference.

b. Municipal Accountant to:

b.1 ensure the accounting records and the RPCPPE are reconciled; and

b.2 exert effort to gather documents and records relative to the untitled and
undocumented parcels of land.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. direct the Chairman of the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the
Municipal Engineer, to prepare and maintain an Inventory of all Local Roads and
a Road Map of the Municipality with the corresponding Road Network ID
Numbers and Road Names in order to observe the yearly preparation of Report
of Local Road Network;

b. create a sub-committee under the Municipal Inventory Committee that will
primarily handle the inventory of Local Road Network System; and

c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to
recognize changes upon completion of the required inventory reports and
reconciliation with the accounting records. Further, depreciation and salvage
value of the assets shall be provided accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the discrepancies
of P6,953,417.92 and P6,112,179.82 between the recorded balances of the Due from
and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their respective reciprocal
accounts in the books of the Province; and subsequently prepare the necessary
adjusting journal entries to present the true balance of the said accounts in the
financial statements of the Municipality. Likewise, initiate the collection and
settlement for the receivables and payables, respectively, found to be valid.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer in
coordination with the Municipal Accountant to produce and submit to the Audit
Team all the unsubmitted 492 DVs without further delay. Otherwise, the said
transactions without DVs and supporting documents shall be disallowed in audit.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to:
 conduct a complete physical count of all the Municipality’s property and submit the required report thereon in proper form; and
 ensure the accuracy and completeness of the information of the items inventoried in the RPCPPE, to hasten reconciliation.
b. Municipal Accountant to:
 continue to reconcile, analyze and investigate the noted discrepancies between the book balance and the RPCPPE to fully identify the cause/s of deficiencies and make necessary adjustments;
 adjust the residual value estimates to at least five percent in accordance with PAG 6 of IPSAS 17; and
 review supporting documents and schedules of the General Ledger (GL) and Subsidiary Ledger (SL) balances of all PPE accounts to determine the variance on the amount of Depreciation Expense reflected in the GL and Lapsing Schedule; and
 ensure that PPELCs are complete and updated.
c. Municipal General Services Officer to coordinate with the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to ensure that RPCPPE, property records and accounting records are reconciled.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is in the process of reconciliation of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to: submit immediately the above reports and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate review and verification of the above reports and documents within the same. Henceforth, ensure that the financial reports and financial documents are submitted within the prescribed period as required under COA Circular Nos. 95-006 and 2009-006. Otherwise, any unjustified failure on the part of the official concerned to submit the required reports shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of her salaries until she shall have complied therewith without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against her, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445 (The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines); and b. Determine the cause/s of the delay in the submission of the accounts of the Municipality to the Auditor and find solutions thereto.
observations.managementAction:
The submission of the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) for year 2020 were religiously submitted on the deadline set by the Commission on Audit (COA). And, for the 6 years 2017 and 2018 DVs were for completion.
Cause of the delay is already determined but delay in the submission of reports still occurs due to lockdown.
observations.reason: Some DVs and official receipts are still submitted beyond the reglementary period. While unsubmitted DVs for CYs 2018 and 2017 are reiterated in Observation No. 6, Part 2 of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to verify and reconcile the discrepancies of the recorded balances of the Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their respective reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province; and subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the true balance of the said accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality. Likewise, initiate the collection and settlement for the receivables and payables found to be valid.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have reconciled the balances of Due to and Due from LGUs reciprocal accounts with the Rizal Provincial Government for year 2019 and 2020. The balances in 2018 and below is still subject for further reconciliations which also includes unreconciled balances of Due to and Due from LGUs reciprocal accounts with the Barangay.
observations.reason: Municipality is still working on Prior Year Unreconciled Balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. exhaust all remaining means to locate and substantiate the relevant documents to substantiate the accounts’ balances found in the dormant accounts and during the transition of new Accounting Team and noted these dormant accounts. b. if warranted, request the write-off of the dormant receivable accounts duly supported with the documentary requirements subject to prescribed in Item 8.0 of pertinent COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had an initial analysis of the said accounts and during the 1st month of transition of new Team and noted these dormant accounts.
observations.reason:
Request for write-off was already forwarded to the Audit Team however the same was returned for completion.
Other documents are being gathered to substantiate the dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to undertake thorough review and analysis of the details of the said dormant accounts and reconcile the same with other related GL and prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were already booked under JEV No. 101-2020-09-008 and JEV No. 200-2020-06-003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. make the necessary adjusting journal entry to reclassify the misclassified funds to their proper accounts and to correct the error in recording the disbursements charged to Other Payables instead of Due to NGAs amounting to P648,763.52 and P5,709,359.49, respectively; b. exhaust all efforts to obtain records and documents for the prior years forwarded balances without details; and c. henceforth, ensure to use correct accounting entries in recording transactions to avoid errors and misstatements of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant have checked previous records such as GL and JEVs made from previous years. The Office of the Municipal Accountant has checked previous records and reconciled down to P870,583.85.
observations.reason: Overstatement was already reduced due to the identified adjusting entry made through JEV No. 300-2020-06-007. Prior years’ forwarded balances require further reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. reclassify the above misclassified liability accounts totaling P972,902.41 to their proper accounts; b. record only obligation with proper and complete documentation; and c. analyze and ascertain the validity of outstanding claims without valid obligations. Those already found valid should be paid immediately in order to clear the payable accounts. Otherwise, recorded obligations without valid documentation and which have been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim has been filed should be reverted to Government Equity. Also, require her to maintain/update subsidiary ledgers for all payable accounts for proper control and easy monitoring and evaluation of claims.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were already reclassified to their proper account as per JEV No. 101-2020-09-008 and JEV No. 200-2020-06-003. Obligations were only recorded based on awarded contracts. Payables already reverted. Payables were analyzed and paid where valid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. send demand letters to AOs with long overdue cash advances and henceforth, ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year to record the related expenses and return the excess cash advance to report the correct cash and expenses as of year-end; b. refrain from processing/granting cash advances to AOs with existing/outstanding cash advance/s; c. ensure that all cash advances are granted within the maximum accountability of the AOs fidelity bond; d. record the unrecorded cash advances and require its liquidation; and e. reconcile the subsidiary ledger balances to eliminate the abnormal/negative balances.
observations.managementAction: Adherence to COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant of Cash Advance is being adhered. Relaxation of said provision was allowed due to pandemic. Abnormal/negative balances were already adjusted and recorded as expense was corrected. Demand letters were already sent to all Accountable Officers with unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the Audit Team all the monthly CRAAF for CY 2019 and henceforth, strictly comply with the cited rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted the CRAAF for CY 2019 as required. Henceforth, compliance will be observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-General Services Officer to strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 656 and COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 for the guidelines in the insurance of government properties with the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Head-GSO provided the initial listing of Building that should be insured. They have coordinated with GSIS for the insurance processing. Target to insure the buildings starting this Year 2021.
observations.reason: Not all insurable properties coverage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the reconciliation of the accounts Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth to identify the status/composition and other details of the unremitted balance and remit immediately to the BIR; expedite the process of resolving issues concerning GSIS contribution/loan payments of municipal employees to immediately remit the amount due to GSIS; and henceforth, strictly adhere with the prescribed period of remittances of the withheld taxes, contribution/loan payments to the various agencies to avoid the payment of interest, surcharges and penalties and the non-granting of loans and/or benefits to the Municipal officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Composition of prior year's obligation is being reduced. The Due to GSIS account was already reconciled. Current taxes withheld were remitted on time. Due to GSIS part of the unremitted balance is subject for representation with GSIS due to system error. The Accounting Office is analyzing and reconciling prior years’ balances.
observations.reason: Prior years’ unremitted balance of Due to BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth represent prior years’ unremitted balance that require further reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Market Administrator to enforce the signing of contracts with stallholders and the payment of stall rights and daily cash tickets. Likewise, send demand letters to all delinquent stallholders for the payment of stall rights. The LCE assigned the OIC-Market Administrator/Coordinator and the Planning Office to conduct an Inventory Report of the Stallholders and Stall Areas.
observations.managementAction: Previous contracts were considered void since there were breach of contract committed by the stallholders. The LCE assigned the OIC-Market Administrator/Coordinator and the Planning Office to conduct an Inventory Report of the Stallholders and Stall Areas.
observations.reason: No copies of contracts with Stallholders received by the Audit Team as requested.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned Municipal Officials to pursue the timely and full implementation of all the programs and activities embodied in the 20% development fund so that the benefits would be delivered to its intended beneficiaries at the soonest possible time.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive have instructed the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor and vigorously pursue the timely and full implementation of all the programs and activities embodied in the 20% Development Fund. Both Municipal Development Office and the Engineering Office were fully informed on the recommendation of the Audit Team. As such, both offices are already adhering on the monitoring and timely implementation of the 20% DF PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person together with the current GAD System (GFPS) to facilitate the establishment of the GAD database as basis for planning and programming of PPAs and formulating policies that are gender-responsive.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to:
 conduct a complete physical count of all the Municipality’s property and
submit the required report thereon in proper form; and
 ensure the accuracy and completeness of the information of the items
inventoried in the RPCPPE, to hasten reconciliation.
b. Municipal Accountant to:
 continue to reconcile, analyze and investigate the noted discrepancy
between the book balance and the RPCPPE to fully identify the
errors/deficiencies that contributed to the noted variance, and make the
necessary adjustments;
 adjust the residual value estimates to at least five percent in accordance with
Paragraph 6 of IPSAS 17;
 review supporting documents and schedules of the General Ledger (GL) and
Subsidiary Ledger (SL) balances of all PPE accounts to determine the
variance on the amount of Depreciation Expense reflected in the GL and
Lapsing Schedule; and
 ensure that PPELCs are complete and updated.
c. Municipal General Services Officer to coordinate with the Inventory Committee
and Municipal Accountant to ensure that RPCPPE, property records and
accounting records are reconciled.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to submit immediately the above reports
and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate review and
verification of the same. Henceforth, ensure that the financial reports and documents
are submitted within the prescribed period as required under COA Circular Nos. 95-
006 and 2009-006. Otherwise, any unjustified failure on the part of the official
concerned to submit the required reports shall be considered a ground for the
automatic suspension of payment of her salaries until she shall have complied
therewith without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against
her, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445 (The Government Auditing Code of the
Philippines); and
b. Determine the cause/s of the delay in the submission of the accounts of the
Municipality to the Auditor and find solutions thereto.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and
Treasurer to verify the discrepancies of P6,060,415.58 and P7,094,409.01 between
the recorded balances of the Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the
Municipality and their respective reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province;
and subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the
true balance of the said accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality.
Likewise, initiate the collection and settlement for the receivables and payables
found to be valid.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. exhaust all remaining means to locate and retrieve the relevant documents to
substantiate the accounts’ balances reported in the financial statements; and
b. if warranted, request the write-off of the dormant receivable accounts duly
supported with the documentary requirements prescribed in Item 8.0 of
COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to undertake thorough review and analysis of the details of the said dormant
accounts and reconcile the same with other related accounts in the trial balance
and prepare necessary adjusting entry/ies, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. make the necessary adjusting journal entry to reclassify the misclassified funds
to their proper accounts and to correct the error in recording the disbursements
charged to Other Payables instead of Due to NGAs account amounting to
P648,763.52 and P5,709,359.49, respectively;
b. exhaust all efforts to obtain records and documents for the prior years
forwarded balances without details amounting to P1,427,351.63; and
c. henceforth, ensure to use correct accounting entries in recording transactions
to avoid misstatements of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. reclassify the above misclassified liability accounts totaling P972,902.41 to their
proper accounts;
b. record only obligation with proper and complete documentation; and
c. analyze and ascertain the validity of outstanding obligations. Those found valid
should be paid immediately in order to clear the payable accounts. Otherwise,
recorded obligations without valid documentation and which have been
outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim has been
filed should be reverted to Government Equity. Also, require her to
maintain/update subsidiary ledgers for all payable accounts for proper control
and easy monitoring and evaluation of claims.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. send demand letters to AOs with long overdue cash advances and henceforth,
ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year to record
the related expenses and return the excess cash advance to report the correct
cash and related expenses as of year-end;
b. refrain from processing/granting cash advances to AOs with
existing/outstanding cash advance/s;
c. ensure that all cash advances are granted within the maximum accountability of
the AOs fidelity bond;
d. record the unrecorded cash advances and require its liquidation; and
e. reconcile the subsidiary ledger balances to eliminate the abnormal/negative
balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer
to immediately submit to the Audit Team all the monthly CRAAF for CY 2019 and
henceforth, strictly comply with the cited rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned Municipal
Officials to submit copies of the contracts and purchase orders within five days
from issuance as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, together with the
pertinent documents, which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial
review, and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-General Services
Officer to strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 656 and COA Circular
No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 for the guidelines in the insurance of government
properties with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. exhaust all means to determine the composition and other details of the prior
year’s obligation of the Due to BIR account and remit immediately to the BIR;
b. expedite the process of resolving issues concerning GSIS contribution/loan
payments of municipal employees to immediately remit the amount due to GSIS;
and
c. henceforth, strictly adhere with the prescribed period of remittances of the
withheld taxes and contributions/loan payments to various concerned agencies,
to avoid the payment of interests, surcharges and penalties and the non-
granting of loans and/or benefits to the officials and employees of the
Municipality

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Market Administrator to
enforce signing of contracts with existing stallholders and the payment of stall
rights and daily cash tickets. Likewise, send demand letters to all delinquent
stallholders for the payment of stall rights.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned Municipal
Officials to:
a. prepare the Local Council for the Protection of Children Annual Work and
Financial Plan as required by the abovementioned law, rules and regulations;
b. submit the Accomplishment Report on the implementation of LCPC PPAs in the
prescribed form to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to
closely monitor and vigorously pursue the timely and full implementation of all the
programs and activities embodied in the 20% development fund so that the benefits
would be delivered to its intended beneficiaries at the soonest possible time.

OIC-Municipal Accountant to:  provide a detailed lapsing schedule to support the PPE balances in the corresponding financial statements and to support the computed depreciation for the current year; and c. OIC-Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain a complete and updated PPE and Real Property Ledger Cards, respectively, to facilitate inventory taking and periodic reconciliation between the property and accounting records and to arrive at the correct balances of the PPE accounts.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile
their records regularly, and effect the necessary adjustments to avoid
accumulation thereof and come up with the correct balance of the Cash in
Bank account in their respective records;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entry to record
the unrecorded deposits which caused the increase in discrepancy
between the bank and book balance of SEF LBP Account No. 1142-1018-38;
and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to justify the adjusting entry made directly in the
Cash in Bank account under the TF by providing pertinent documents to
support the same, otherwise, the adjustment made should be reversed
immediately.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the required
certified list of taxpayers and the tax collectible from each at the beginning of the
year to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording the RPT/SET Receivable
in the Municipality’s books of accounts.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
maintain a complete set of SL of the taxpayers per Real Property Unit (RPU) to
serve as basis on the reported balances of the RPT/SET Receivable accounts in
the financial statement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to disclose the required information in the Notes to Financial
Statements to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to
provide them with additional relevant information on the pertinent accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial
Accountant and Treasurer to verify the discrepancies of P19,331,479.39 and
P10,787,829.47 between the recorded balances of the Due from and Due to LGUs
accounts of the Municipality and their respective reciprocal accounts Due to and
Due from LGUs account reflected in the books of the Province; and subsequently
prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the true balance of the
said accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality. Likewise, strictly
enforce the collection and settlement for the receivables and payables found to be
valid.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the:
a. Municipal officials and employees with outstanding cash advances to
immediately liquidate/settle their accountabilities. If warranted, impose the
withholding of salaries of those who fail or refuse to comply with the
regulations;
b. Department Heads to require the settlement of and clearance from
accountabilities of all municipal officials and employees prior to the
acceptance of their resignations; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to certify each claim for cash advance that the
person drawing such has no outstanding previous cash advances prior to
processing the claim.
We also recommended that the LCE consider filing a request for authority to write-
off the dormant unliquidated cash advance of the late former Mayor of the
Municipality, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to:
a. make a detailed review of the account in coordination with the Human
Resource Management Office (HRMO) to determine the cause of the
discrepancy and make the necessary adjusting entry thereof; and
b. request from the GSIS an updated statement of accounts of the Municipality so
that they could settle immediately any outstanding liability with the System. If
proven none, the Municipality should trace and return any outstanding amount
withheld to the municipal employees, if still possible, or revert the same to
Government Equity account.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
a. stop approving disbursements which are not included in the list of allowable
expenses chargeable against SEF pursuant to the DepEd, DBM, and DILG Joint
Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and
b. direct the Municipal Budget Officer to replenish the SEF from the General Fund
in the amount expended for unauthorized SEF expenditures. Otherwise, the
said transactions shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road
map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and
reporting of the Municipality’s road network system;
b. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components
for road projects; and thereafter
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks equivalent to
the required transition provision percentage every year starting 2016 up to
2019.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
require the BAC to submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the
GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period in
accordance with the provision of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We strongly reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive direct the concerned officials to:
a. prepare and submit to the Audit Team at the beginning of the year a list of all
on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the
year; and
b. inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project
signboards and/or public notices are already posted as required under the
above-cited circular to promote transparency and accountability regarding the
PPAs undertaken by the Municipality and facilitate the validation of the same
by the Audit Team.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. Inventory Committee to complete the inventory count to prove existence of the Municipality’s properties, and submit the required report thereon to the Auditor, pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I. Also, the inventory report should be reconciled with the accounting and property records. Any discrepancies found should be investigated, and the necessary adjustments should be made where the error was committed; and b. Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to maintain complete and updated PPE and Real Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate inventory taking and periodic reconciliation between the property and accounting records and to arrive at the correct balances of the PPE accounts.
